#include <span>

struct UserData {
    uint64_t user_id;
    uint8_t age;
    std::string name;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream(user_id, age, name);
    }
};

UserData deserialize_user_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    // Remove constness from the buffer
    std::span<uint8_t> non_const_buffer = const_cast<std::span<uint8_t>>(buffer);

    // Create a buffer adaptor for the non-const buffer
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::span<uint8_t>> adaptor(non_const_buffer);

    // Create a binary stream with the adaptor
    hexi::binary_stream<decltype(adaptor)> stream(adaptor);

    // Create a UserData object and deserialize into it
    UserData user_data;
    stream.deserialise(user_data);

    return user_data;
}

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct DataResult {
    uint8_t data_type;
    std::vector<uint32_t> int_items;
    std::vector<float> float_items;
};

DataResult preview_and_read_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    // Remove constness from the buffer because buffer_adaptor and binary_stream require non-const?
    auto non_const_buffer = const_cast<std::span<uint8_t>&>(buffer);

    // Step 1: Create a buffer_adaptor from the non_const_buffer
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::span<uint8_t>> adaptor(non_const_buffer);

    // Step 2: Preview the first byte (data_type) without advancing the read pointer
    uint8_t data_type;
    adaptor.copy(&data_type, 1); // This copies 1 byte without advancing

    // Step 5: Create a binary_stream from the buffer_adaptor
    hexi::binary_stream<decltype(adaptor)> stream(adaptor);

    // Step 6: Read the item_count (uint32_t) from the binary_stream
    uint32_t item_count;
    stream >> item_count;

    // Step 3: Calculate the total data size needed for items: item_count * sizeof(item) based on data_type
    size_t item_size = (data_type == 0) ? sizeof(uint32_t) : sizeof(float);
    size_t total_item_size = item_count * item_size;

    // Step 4: Check if the buffer has enough data for the items
    if (adaptor.size() - 4 < total_item_size) {
        // Not enough data, return default result
        return DataResult{};
    }

    // Step 7: Based on data_type, read the appropriate number of items into the result vector.
    if (data_type == 0) {
        DataResult result{data_type};
        result.int_items.resize(item_count);
        stream.get(result.int_items.data(), item_count); // Read item_count uint32_t
        return result;
    } else {
        DataResult result{data_type};
        result.float_items.resize(item_count);
        stream.get(result.float_items.data(), item_count); // Read item_count float
        return result;
    }
}
```

struct PacketHeader {

    // Check if the stream is still good
    if (!stream.good()) {
        return PacketHeader{};
    }

    // Return the header
    return *header_span;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

using namespace hexi::detail;

template<typename T>
std::size_t encode_varint(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, T value) {
    return varint_encode(writer, value);
}

template<typename T>
T decode_varint(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader) {
    return varint_decode<T>(reader);
}

template<typename T>
bool encode_decode_roundtrip(T original_value) {
    // Create a buffer
    std::vector<std::byte> buffer;
    
    // Create a buffer_adaptor for the buffer
    hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>> adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Create a binary_stream_writer and binary_stream_reader using the adaptor
    hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer writer(adaptor);
    hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader reader(adaptor);
    
    // Encode the original value
    encode_varint(writer, original_value);
    
    // Decode the value
    T decoded_value = decode_varint<T>(reader);
    
    // Compare the decoded value with the original
    return decoded_value == original_value;
}
```</answer>
